spec_match.rationale: The lab now names and models the core spec surfaces: document.prerendering, onprerenderingchange, activationStart, blocked-until-activation, the top-level prerender-cross-origin-frames token, and the iframe credentialed-prerender response gate.

interactive.rationale: Live browser probes, multiple select controls, a script-mode toggle, reset/copy actions, and an activation replay all update the decision matrix and request trace in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept now delivers the feature-index promise by probing prerender state, checking activation timing, explaining the Supports-Loading-Mode header pattern, and showing compatibility decisions.

edge_cases.rationale: The scenario builder covers missing or wrong headers, same-origin versus cross-origin frames, same-site versus cross-site caveats, non-trustworthy targets, non-HTTP(S) iframes, redirects, intrusive API work, and unsupported-browser fallback.

references.rationale: The references section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5112398709129216.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables for color, keeps button background and color overrides paired, and avoids the static-card pattern.

summary: The compatibility lab now behaves as a realistic prerender eligibility and fallback tool for Chrome 147 cross-origin iframe behavior, with no remaining high-priority critique items for this concept.
